summary.intent: Analyze PR #2587 (PyGithub 2.x upgrade + GitHub App auth) to identify minimal Jenkins jobs needed to test all code paths, then run those jobs to validate the authentication changes work correctly without using GITHUB_TOKEN.
summary.outcome: Identified 3 core auth mechanisms (PyGithub REST client, GIT_ASKPASS for git CLI, URL normalization) and minimal job set (ocp4-scan-konflux, gen-assembly, build-fbc). Successfully ran and validated 2 of 3 jobs confirming GitHub App auth working. Discovered and created separate PR to fix security issue where GIT_PASSWORD tokens were being logged in plaintext.

repo: PR #2587 introduces 3 distinct auth mechanisms: get_github_client_for_org (PyGithub), get_github_git_auth_env (GIT_ASKPASS), ensure_github_https_url (URL normalization)
All Jenkins jobs in aos-cd-builds accept ART_TOOLS_COMMIT parameter for testing PR branches
GitHub App installation tokens use 'ghs_' prefix, distinguishing them from PATs
pyartcd pipelines map to Jenkins jobs in aos-cd-builds/build/ with URL-encoded job names
DRY_RUN=true is more reliable than MOCK parameter for safe Jenkins job testing

code.finding: cmd_gather logs entire set_env dict including sensitive values like GIT_PASSWORD in plaintext - security issue
get_github_git_auth_env() returns empty dict {} when no credentials needed, allowing SSH auth to continue
URL rewriting to HTTPS happens before checking if askpass credentials were created, potentially breaking SSH remotes for private repos (flagged by coderabbitai bot)
Exercises all 3 auth mechanisms (M1, M2, M3) making it the richest single file for testing auth changes
GitRepository class wraps get_github_git_auth_env for setup, branch switching, commit/push operations

workflow: Map modified files to Jenkins jobs by understanding which pipelines import those files
Search Jenkins logs for patterns like 'Using GitHub App auth' or 'GITHUB_TOKEN' to verify auth mechanisms
gen-assembly with DRY_RUN=true exits before hitting PyGithub API path, providing only partial coverage
GitHub App auth generates fresh installation tokens per-org during job execution
Jenkins MCP server tree queries may not return job names - need to iterate to find correct URL-encoded path format

friction: PR branch contained many unrelated changes beyond PyGithub upgrade, making diff analysis harder
Initial Jenkins MCP server tree queries didn't return job names, required iteration
gen-assembly DRY_RUN mode exits early, missing PyGithub API coverage
Assistant got stuck twice analyzing massive diffs and needed user redirection
open_items: build-fbc job not yet tested (3rd job in minimal set to complete M2/M3 coverage)
Code review bot flagged potential SSH remote URL rewriting issue (git_helper.py:22-25) - should preserve SSH when get_github_git_auth_env returns empty dict
redact-secrets-in-exectools-logging branch created but PR not yet pushed/created
Need to verify secret redaction fix works across all exectools functions that log env vars, not just cmd_gather
